title: Logistics Accessorials and Proof of Delivery
friction_templates: Accessorial charges disputed due to missing documentation
POD (Proof of Delivery) not captured or transmitted timely
BOL (Bill of Lading) discrepancies causing billing delays
Detention time not properly documented or approved
Appointment scheduling conflicts causing carrier wait times
decision_rights_templates: Dispatcher owns accessorial pre-approval up to $X threshold
Operations manager approves detention over 2 hours
Driver must capture POD within 15 minutes of delivery
Billing team has 24h to dispute accessorial charges
Customer service owns appointment rescheduling communication
stop_doing_templates: Stop accepting verbal accessorial agreements - require written approval
Stop processing invoices without matching POD documentation
Stop allowing drivers to leave without signed BOL confirmation
Stop manual entry of detention times - use automated tracking
Stop batch-processing PODs - transmit in real-time
experiment_templates: Day 1-2: Implement real-time POD capture requirement for all deliveries
Day 3-5: Track detention time automatically from arrival to departure
Day 6-7: Review accessorial disputes and identify documentation gaps
